archive.description: Bundle multiple files into a single archive

folder_structure.description: flat = all files in root, by_job = subfolder per source job

audio_overlay.description:
Mix a secondary audio asset (overlay) over a primary audio or
video base. Use cases: DJ tags, podcast intros/outros, station
IDs, jingles, branded mid-rolls.

**Industry naming.** This is "audio overlay" or "audio branding"
— NOT "audio watermark". Audio watermarking is steganographic
(imperceptible identifier embedded in audio for ownership /
forensic tracking) and is owned by the separate `audio_watermark`
operation (planned per ticket I20).

Multi-input (Path B with `role: base` + `role: overlay` per
`JobInputV2.role`). `min_inputs: 2 = max_inputs: 2` for V2.0;
multi-overlay support is advertised as a planned feature
(`features.multi_overlay_stack`) but Lambda-side implementation
deferred.

The overlay can be uploaded directly, imported externally, or
referenced from a vault connection per `WorkflowSource` (from
I10). The base is whatever single-asset source the workflow
upstream has — for `mime_groups.audio`, the base is the primary
audio track; for `mime_groups.video`, the overlay is mixed into
the video's audio track and the visual track passes through
untouched.

Per ADR-0001 §1.3 (Tension 1 — `planned` operations return
`feature_not_available` 422 until Lambda support ships). Per
ADR-0004 §"V2 JobDefinition" + plan v5 §F1 round 6.

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via
`JobInputV2.per_input_options`. Lambda-side support not yet
confirmed; tagged `planned` per ADR-0001 §1.4. Mirrors the
same-named feature on `image_watermark`.

sync_to_silence.description:
Anchor overlay placement to detected silence regions in the
base track (e.g. drop a podcast jingle into the natural
breath after the host says "back in a sec"). Server-side
silence detection runs at workflow-create time; the actual
overlay start position is reported on
`OperationMetrics.overlay_anchor_ms`. Gates two extra
options: `silence_threshold_db` and `min_silence_ms`. Lambda
support not yet shipped.

duck_attack_ms.description:
Time (milliseconds) for the ducker to reach full
attenuation once the overlay crosses `duck_threshold`.
Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_ratio.description:
Side-chain compression ratio. Higher values produce more
aggressive ducking. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_release_ms.description:
Time (milliseconds) for the ducker to release back to
unity gain after the overlay drops below
`duck_threshold`. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_threshold.description:
Side-chain threshold (dB). Base audio is ducked when the
overlay exceeds this level. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duration.description:
Maximum duration (seconds) of the overlay AS PLAYED — the
overlay asset is trimmed to this length if longer. Omit
(or set 0) to play the overlay's full natural length.
**Disambiguation:** this is overlay-trim duration, NOT
output duration. The output duration always matches the
base track's duration.

fade_in_ms.description:
Linear fade-in applied to the overlay (milliseconds).
Default 0 = hard start.

fade_out_ms.description:
Linear fade-out applied to the overlay (milliseconds).
Default 0 = hard stop.

loop.description:
When true, the overlay loops continuously across the
duration of the base track (or up to the trimmed
`duration`). Useful for ambient bed music / background
stings.

loop_interval.description:
Silence gap (seconds) inserted between overlay loops.
Default 0 = back-to-back. Only applies when `loop: true`.

min_silence_ms.description:
Minimum silence duration (milliseconds) to qualify as an
anchor region for `sync_to_silence`. Inert until that
feature flips to stable.

mode.description:
How the overlay combines with the base audio:
- `mix`: simple sum (overlay + base; both audible).
- `duck`: side-chain compress the base under the overlay
  (base attenuates while overlay plays — broadcast pattern).
- `replace`: mute the base for the overlay's duration
  (overlay only; useful for hard-cut station IDs).

overlay_gain_db.description:
Gain applied to the overlay in decibels before mixing.
Default -3 dB matches typical podcast intro / DJ-tag
level. Positive values amplify (clip risk); negative
values attenuate. Clamped to range.

silence_threshold_db.description:
Silence threshold (dB) for the `sync_to_silence` feature.
Base audio below this level for at least `min_silence_ms`
qualifies as a silence region into which the overlay can
be anchored. Inert until
`features.sync_to_silence.availability` flips to stable.

start_at.description:
Start position (seconds) on the base track at which the
overlay begins playing. Default 0.0 = mix from the
beginning. Inert when
`features.sync_to_silence.availability` flips to
`stable` and `mode: sync_to_silence` is selected
(placement is then anchored to detected silence).

duck_attack_ms.description: Ducker attack (milliseconds). Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_ratio.description: Side-chain compression ratio. Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_release_ms.description: Ducker release (milliseconds). Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duck_threshold.description: Side-chain threshold (dB). Only applies when `mode: duck`.

duration.description:
Maximum duration (seconds) of the overlay AS PLAYED. The
overlay asset is trimmed to this length if longer. Output
video duration always matches the base.

fade_in_ms.description: Linear fade-in applied to the overlay (milliseconds).

fade_out_ms.description: Linear fade-out applied to the overlay (milliseconds).

loop.description:
When true, the overlay loops continuously across the
duration of the base video.

min_silence_ms.description:
Minimum silence duration (milliseconds) for
`sync_to_silence`. Inert until that feature flips to
stable.

mode.description:
Same modes as the audio mime_group. `replace` mutes the
base video's audio for the overlay's duration.

no_audio_track_behaviour.description:
Controls behaviour when the base video has no audio
track:
- `reject`: workflow-create returns 422 (default — surfaces
  the problem at the API edge rather than producing a
  surprising silent-base output).
- `create_silent`: synthesise a silent base track at the
  video's framerate and mix the overlay into that.
- `replace`: same as `create_silent` but signals intent
  ("the overlay IS the audio track"), letting Lambda
  skip the base-track synthesis when the overlay covers
  the full video duration.

overlay_gain_db.description: Gain applied to the overlay in decibels before mixing.

silence_threshold_db.description:
Silence threshold (dB) for `sync_to_silence`. Inert
until that feature flips to stable.

start_at.description:
Start position (seconds) on the base video's audio track
at which the overlay begins playing.

audio_to_video.description:
Produce a video from an audio input plus an OPTIONAL still image
overlay. Use cases: podcast → YouTube uploads, audio-only tracks
that need a video container for platforms requiring it, branded
audio releases with a static cover image.

**Role asymmetry.** First role-based operation on the contract
with an OPTIONAL role — REQUIRED `base` (audio source) +
OPTIONAL `overlay` (still image, 0..1). When `overlay` is
omitted, the video uses a solid `background_color`. The
machine-readable cardinality lives in `per_role_cardinality`
on this schema (per ADR-0015). API consumption site:
`OperationInputRoleValidator` (rule table) consumed by
`SchemaValidationService.php` in `compression_api`.

**Input vs output asymmetry.** The single `mime_groups.audio`
block keys the INPUT MIME family (the `base` role input is
audio). The OUTPUT is always video — `output_format` controls
the container; `mime_groups` is NOT the output type. Mirrors
the `audio_overlay.video` group pattern (input-keyed).

Re-encode required (audio → video composition always re-encodes).
Lower framerate + FFmpeg `-tune stillimage` keeps file size
tiny when the overlay is a still image (the common case).

Per ticket [`SlluxMBN`](https://trello.com/c/SlluxMBN) +
ADR-0015. `availability: planned` until the cross-repo Lambda
support ships; the API rejects workflow-create with
`feature_not_available` (422) until then.

background_color.description:
Hex `#RRGGBB` solid background colour. Used only when
`overlay` is omitted (no image input). When an `overlay`
image is provided, the background colour is invisible
behind the image (subject to `image_fit`).

framerate.description:
Output video framerate. Default `2` fps — combined with
FFmpeg `-tune stillimage`, keeps the file size tiny for
the common case of a single still overlay. Raise for
animated overlays (not supported in V1 — overlay must be
a still image) or platform requirements (e.g. YouTube
recommends 24+ fps; some platforms accept lower).

image_fit.description:
How to fit the overlay image into the output frame.
`contain` = letterbox (preserve aspect ratio, may show
background); `cover` = crop (fill the frame, may crop
the image); `stretch` = distort to fit (preserves no
aspect ratio). Default `contain` preserves the image
aesthetic.

output_format.description:
Output container format. V1 supports `mp4` only. Future
additions (webm, mov) await Lambda follow-up.

output_resolution.description:
Output video resolution. `hd_720p` = 1280×720 (default),
`fhd_1080p` = 1920×1080, `sd_480p` = 854×480. Choose
lower for small file size; higher when the overlay image
quality warrants it.

audio_watermark.description:
Embed a steganographic forensic watermark into an audio asset (or
a video's audio track). The watermark is psychoacoustically masked
to be inaudible and engineered to survive lossy compression,
transcoding, broadcast chains, and analog-hole capture (Cinavia /
Resemble PerTh territory).

**Industry naming.** This is *audio watermarking* (steganographic;
used for ownership, forensic tracking, content provenance) — NOT
*audio overlay* (audible mixing of secondary audio for branding).
Audio overlay is owned by the separate `audio_overlay` operation
(per ticket I19).

Single-input. Watermark embedding takes the base asset and writes
out an indistinguishable-to-the-ear copy with the encoded payload.

**Decode endpoint.** A paired `POST /api/audio-watermark/decode`
endpoint extracts the embedded watermark from a previously-marked
asset, returning `{watermark_id, payload?, confidence, method,
detected_at}`. Decode is **own-watermarks-only** (rate-limited
+ audited; the decoder will refuse to extract from media the
caller did not mark themselves) — pin per spike S11 acceptance.

Per ADR-0001 §1.3 (Tension 1 — `planned` operations return
`feature_not_available` 422 until Lambda support ships).
Per plan v5 §F1 round 6 (codex narrowed methods enum from 5 to 3,
dropping `spread_spectrum` / `echo_hiding` / `phase_coding` —
textbook families, not product choices).

density.description:
Watermark spatial density along the asset duration:
- `sparse`: payload bits inserted at wider intervals;
  decode confidence drops on short clips.
- `normal` (default): balanced.
- `dense`: maximum decode confidence on short clips at
  the cost of slightly higher perceptual risk.

method.description:
Watermarking algorithm:
- `auto`: server selects the best available method for the
  input (currently maps to `psychoacoustic`; will widen as
  `neural` ships).
- `psychoacoustic`: classical masking-curve embedding.
  Resemble's open-source PerTh library provides the path-
  to-prod reference implementation. Survives MP3/AAC at
  moderate bitrates and broadcast chains.
- `neural`: ML-based embedding (deep generative). Higher
  survival under aggressive transcoding + analog-hole
  capture; longer encoding time + non-deterministic.

payload.description:
Optional caller-supplied payload to embed (max 256
characters). Use cases: ownership marker, distribution
channel ID, license key. When omitted, the server
generates a UUID-v7 `watermark_id` and embeds that
instead; the generated ID is returned on
`OperationResult` so the caller can subsequently call
the decode endpoint to verify.

robustness.description:
Survival profile:
- `light`: minimal payload-strength; smallest perceptual
  footprint. Survives a single re-encode pass at moderate
  bitrate.
- `compression_resistant` (default): survives multiple
  MP3/AAC re-encodes at typical streaming bitrates.
- `broadcast_grade`: survives broadcast compression
  chains, EQ, dynamic-range compression, and analog-hole
  capture (microphone re-recording). Higher embedding
  time + slightly larger perceptual footprint.

density.description: Watermark density along the asset duration.

method.description:
Watermarking algorithm. Same semantics as the `audio`
mime_group; embedded into the video's audio track only.

payload.description:
Optional caller-supplied payload to embed (max 256
characters). When omitted, the server generates a UUID-v7
`watermark_id` and embeds that instead.

robustness.description: Survival profile. Same semantics as audio mime_group.

compress.description: Reduce file size while maintaining acceptable quality

channels.description: Number of audio channels (1 = mono, 2 = stereo)

normalize.description: Apply loudness normalization (EBU R128 / -14 LUFS target)

font_subsetting.description: Subset embedded fonts to only include glyphs used in the document. Significant size reduction for books with large font files

image_quality.description: Recompress embedded images at this quality level

strip_unused_css.description: Remove CSS rules not referenced by any content. Risk: may affect rendering in some readers

image_quality.description: Recompress embedded images at this quality level

strip_metadata.description: Remove document metadata (author, revision history, comments)

strip_unused_styles.description: Remove style definitions not referenced in document content

image_quality.description: Recompress embedded images at this quality level (1 = smallest, 100 = best)

strip_hidden_data.description: Remove revisions, comments, personal info, hidden rows/columns, and speaker notes

strip_macros.description: Remove VBA macros and ActiveX controls

strip_unused_fonts.description: Remove embedded fonts not referenced in the document. Risk: may affect rendering on systems without the font installed

flatten_forms.description: Flatten interactive form fields into static content

pages.description: Page selection (e.g. '1-5,8,10-12'). Omit to keep all pages.

profile.description: Optimization profile. web = aggressive (downsample images, strip metadata), print = preserve quality, archive = PDF/A compliance, max = smallest file

fit.description: Resize mode. max = fit within bounds, crop = fill and trim, scale = force exact dimensions. Only applies when width or height is set.

icc_profile.description: ICC color profile handling. preserve = keep original, strip = remove, srgb = convert to sRGB

metadata.description: Metadata handling. all = strip everything (smallest file), none = keep all EXIF/IPTC/XMP, copyright = keep only copyright/author fields, sensitive = keep EXIF but strip GPS/location (GDPR-friendly)

mode.description: Compression algorithm. lossy = smaller file, lossless = no quality loss, auto = best for format

output_format.description: Output format. original = keep input format, auto = best for browser support (AVIF > WebP > JPEG based on Accept header hint), smallest = try all supported formats and return the smallest file

progressive.description: Enable progressive rendering (JPEG only). Renders low-quality preview first, then refines.

quality.description: Compression quality (1 = smallest file, 100 = best quality). Only applies to lossy mode.

audio_codec.description: Audio codec. aac = widest compatibility, opus = best quality/size, copy = passthrough

codec.description: Video codec. h264 = widest compatibility, h265/av1 = better compression but slower

crf.description: Constant Rate Factor (0 = lossless, 51 = worst, 23 = visually good). Only applies to CRF mode.

encoding_mode.description: crf = constant quality (variable file size), target_size = constrained file size (two-pass)

faststart.description: Move MP4 moov atom to start for progressive web playback (MP4 only)

fit.description: Resize mode. max = fit within bounds (preserves ratio), crop/scale/pad = exact dimensions. Only applies when width or height is set.

preset.description: Encoding speed vs compression ratio trade-off

target_size_bytes.description: Target output file size in bytes (min 1MB). Two-pass encoding to meet this target.

convert.description: Convert file to a different format

bitrate.description: Output bitrate in kbps (lossy formats only)

output_format.description: Target image format (renders PDF pages as images)

pages.description: Page selection (e.g. '1-5,8'). Each page produces a separate output file.

background.description: Background colour (hex, e.g. '#ffffff') for transparent images converted to JPEG

codec.description: Video codec for target format. Defaults to format's standard codec.

custom_luma.description:
Apply a caller-uploaded luma matte (transition-mask) over a base
video to produce a custom luma-matte transition effect. Inspired
by Cloudinary's deprecated luma-matte custom transitions and
intended as a paid-tier (`pro`+) creative feature.

**NOT** related to FFmpeg's `xfade=custom` filter (which is an
expression-mode parameter on the same `xfade` filter). The
`merge.transition.custom` value is tagged `planned` with
documentation_url pointing at this operation; once `custom_luma`
ships, the merge slot is repurposed to a "use the dedicated
`custom_luma` operation instead" advisory.

Multi-input: exactly one input with `role: base` (the source
video being transitioned) + exactly one input with
`role: transition_mask` (the luma matte — a video whose pixel
luminance drives the transition reveal — bright = revealed,
dark = held). The mask can be uploaded directly, imported
externally, or referenced from a vault connection per
`WorkflowSource` (from I10).

Per ADR-0001 §1.3 (Tension 1 — `planned` operations return
`feature_not_available` HTTP 422 until Lambda support ships).
Per ADR-0004 §"V2 JobDefinition" + plan v5 §F4 round 6
(codex-corrected — `xfade=custom` is an expression, not a
transition; this operation is the deliberate alternative).

duration.description:
Transition duration in seconds. The mask is time-stretched
or trimmed to this duration when applied.

mask_blur.description:
Gaussian blur radius (pixels) applied to the luma matte
before compositing. Softens the transition edge. Default
0.0 = no blur. Larger values produce a more diffused
transition.

mask_contrast.description:
Contrast multiplier applied to the luma matte before
compositing. Sharpens (>1) or softens (<1) the
transition edge. Default 1.0 = no contrast adjustment.

mask_invert.description:
When true, inverts the luma matte (bright ↔ dark). Lets
callers reuse a single mask asset for both reveal and
hide directions.

start_offset.description:
Offset (seconds) from the base clip start at which the
transition begins. The transition runs for `duration`
seconds starting at `start_offset`.

image_watermark.description:
Apply an image overlay (file or external source) onto a base media
asset. Multi-input: exactly one input with role: base (the source
asset) + exactly one with role: overlay (the watermark). The overlay
can be an upload, an external_import handle, or a vault connection
per WorkflowSource (ticket I10).

Stable today for static-image bases (image/jpeg, image/png,
image/webp); animated GIF bases are advertised as `planned` via
the parallel `image_gif` mime_group — schema is contract-defined,
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until Lambda
support ships. Per Tension 1 (ADR-0001) + plan v5 §F4 round 6.

**Video bases are NOT supported by `image_watermark`.** Use the
dedicated `video_watermark` operation
(`schemas/operations/video_watermark.yaml`) which routes through
FFmpeg overlay + re-encode. Per ADR-0013.

**Audio is explicitly NOT supported.** For audio overlay (DJ tags,
podcast intros, jingles), use the `audio_overlay` operation
(declared in schemas/operations/audio_overlay.yaml at
`availability: planned`).

Source capped at 25 megapixels for image bases (V1 watermark
precedent retained); per-frame caps for animated GIF bases will be
defined when the corresponding Lambda support ships.
Per ADR-0004 §"V2 JobDefinition" + plan v5 §F1.

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via JobInputV2.per_input_options.
Lambda-side support not yet confirmed; tagged planned per
ADR-0001 §1.4. Scope is image-base only at the time this feature
was specified; extension to GIF / video bases tracks alongside
the corresponding mime_group `planned` → `stable` flips.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position on the base image. Mirrors
Mux/Cloudinary positioning. Combined with margin_x and
margin_y for fine-grained offset from the anchor point.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as either pixels (e.g.
"40px") or percentage of base image width (e.g. "5%").
Direction is anchor-relative — positive moves toward image
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** When the anchor is `center`
the centre is the centre — there is no anchor-relative
direction. Non-zero `margin_x` (anything other than the
default `"0px"` or the equivalent `"0%"`) combined with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422). To nudge a
roughly-centred overlay, pick a peripheral anchor (e.g.
`top_left`) and use `margin_x` / `margin_y` to position it.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as either pixels (e.g.
"20px") or percentage of base image height (e.g. "3%").
Direction is anchor-relative — positive moves toward image
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as
`margin_x` — non-zero `margin_y` with `anchor: center` is
rejected by the API at workflow-create time as
`invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

opacity.description:
Overlay opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).
Clamped to range.

overlay_width.description:
Optional overlay width as either pixels (e.g. "200px") or
percentage of base image width (e.g. "30%"). Aspect ratio is
preserved. Omit to use the original overlay size.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position on the base GIF (applied uniformly
across frames). Same semantics as the `image` group.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor in pixels or percentage of
base width. Anchor-relative; positive moves toward the
frame centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as the
`image` mime_group — non-zero `margin_x` with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor in pixels or percentage of
base height. Anchor-relative; positive moves toward the
frame centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as the
`image` mime_group — non-zero `margin_y` with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

opacity.description:
Overlay opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).
Clamped to range.

overlay_width.description:
Optional overlay width as pixels or percentage of base
width. Aspect ratio preserved. Omit to use the original
overlay size.

gap_duration.description: Silence duration between clips in seconds

normalize_audio.description: Normalize audio levels across all clips

output_type.description: Output format for audio merge (concatenation). Currently fixed at 'audio'. Optional — API injects this default post-validation so callers may omit it.

transition.description:
Default transition between clips. Audio-only catalog
(acrossfade-backed); see FORMAT.md §"Audio vs visual
transition catalogs (merge)" for additions policy. Future
values (e.g. `fade_curve` variants) will be added with
`per_value_availability` per ADR-0001 §1.4.

crossfade_duration.description: Override crossfade duration for this join point

gap_duration.description: Override gap duration for this join point

transition.description:
Override transition for the join point preceding this
input. Audio-only catalog (acrossfade-backed); see
FORMAT.md §"Audio vs visual transition catalogs (merge)".

output_type.description: Result format: animated GIF or slideshow video. No default — caller must pick explicitly (image/collage and document/PDF concat are not supported by the V1 merge Lambda).

transition.description:
Transition effect between images in the slideshow output.
Catalog from FFmpeg 7.1.1 xfade filter (per ADR-0007). See
per_value_availability for per-value rollout state. The
`none` value is a sentinel ("no transition / hard cut"),
not an FFmpeg preset.

transition_duration.description: Duration of transition effect in seconds

codec.description:
Output codec when merge re-encodes. Inert when stream-copy
concat is selected (re_encode_mode=auto + compatible inputs).

crf.description: Constant Rate Factor when encoding_mode=crf. Lower = higher quality / larger file.

encoding_mode.description:
CRF (quality-targeted) vs target_size (two-pass to fixed file size).
`target_size` is currently `codec=h264` only — libx265 / libvpx-vp9 / libsvtav1 each
require a different two-pass invocation and are tracked as separate per-codec
Lambda follow-ups. The contract narrows `target_size_bytes.depends_on` with
`codec: [h264]`, which rejects `encoding_mode=target_size + codec ∈ {h265, vp9, av1}`
as `invalid_options` **when `target_size_bytes` is present in the request**. Fully
gating `encoding_mode=target_size` itself on codec (so that omitting
`target_size_bytes` is also rejected) awaits a `per_value_depends_on` schema
vocab extension (ticket bsV3FWM5); Lambda continues to catch the residual
`target_size + non-h264 + no target_size_bytes` case in the meantime.

normalize_audio.description: Normalize audio levels across all clips

output_type.description: Output format for video merge (concatenation). Currently fixed at 'video'. Optional — API injects this default post-validation so callers may omit it.

re_encode_mode.description:
Re-encode policy for video concat.
- `auto`: probe inputs and choose stream-copy concat (when
  codec/container/timebase/framerate/resolution/pixel-format
  match) or full re-encode.
- `always`: force re-encode (use when caller wants
  normalised output options).
- `never`: return 422 `REQUIRES_REENCODE` if inputs are not
  concat-compatible.
Compatibility algorithm is server-side and deliberately
opaque (per plan v5 §F8.2). The actual path taken is
reported on `OperationMetrics.re_encode_decision`.

target_resolution.description: Output dimensions WxH (e.g. "1920x1080"). Omit to inherit from inputs.

target_size_bytes.description:
Two-pass target file size in bytes (minimum 1 MiB). Required when
`encoding_mode=target_size`. h264-only today; see `encoding_mode` description
for per-codec rollout status.

Practical floor: a ~100 kbps post-AAC video bitrate (i.e. `target_size_bytes`
corresponding to less than ~100 kbps of video after subtracting the 160 kbps
AAC audio track over the merged duration) is silently floored by libx264 and
the resulting file will not honour the requested `target_size_bytes`.
Publishers targeting very small outputs should size accordingly.

transition.description:
Default transition between clips. Catalog from FFmpeg 7.1.1
xfade filter (per ADR-0007). See per_value_availability for
per-value rollout state.

crossfade_duration.description: Override crossfade duration for this join point

transition.description: Override transition for the join point preceding this input.

split.description:
Fan one input file into N outputs across GIF, PDF, audio, and
video MIME families. Single-input — one input file per job, fanned
into N outputs per the per-mime-group catalog. Mirrors the
`merge` / `convert` catalog-split-by-mime-group pattern.

**Three modes** (audio + video; the mode discriminator gates which
of the three options applies per the `depends_on` rules in this
schema):
- `interval`: split every N numeric-seconds.
- `count`: split into N equal-duration pieces (integer 2..=200).
- `cut_points`: explicit cut points in numeric-seconds (strictly
  increasing, no duplicates, each > 0 and < probed duration).

**GIF + PDF use range-based selection** instead of three modes:
- GIF (`image_gif`): `frame_range` REQUIRED + `output_format`
  enum {png,webp,jpg}.
- PDF (`document_pdf`): `page_range` OR `page_groups` (mutually
  exclusive per `depends_on`).

**Wire format**: numeric seconds (floats allowed for sub-second
precision). NOT ISO 8601 duration strings. Matches FFmpeg /
Cloudinary / Shotstack / AWS MediaConvert conventions.

**200-output hard cap** per ADR-0009 §D5
(`OperationResult.outputs[].maxItems: 200`). Preflight rejects
requests exceeding this BEFORE work as `invalid_options` (422).
Cap math by mode:
- `interval`: `ceil(probed_duration / interval) <= 200`
- `count`: enforced by the option's `min: 2 max: 200` range
- `cut_points`: `cut_points.len() + 1 <= 200`
- PDF: resolved page count after expansion `<= 200`
  (cross-ref [`WgCqnMRa`](https://trello.com/c/WgCqnMRa))
- GIF: resolved frame count after expansion `<= 200`

**Output naming**: 3-digit zero-padded — `output-001` ..
`output-200`. Matches the convert PDF→N precedent.

**Output envelope binding** (per ADR-0014 + ADR-0009 §D2):
- `image_gif`: `PositionIndexed` (frame stream-position 0-based
  ordinal)
- `document_pdf`: `PageIndexed` (1-based gapless page index)
- `audio`: `PositionIndexed` (cut stream-position ordinal)
- `video`: `PositionIndexed` (cut stream-position ordinal)

**`precision` flag** (audio + video only): `fast` (default) =
packet-boundary approximate cuts; `exact` = re-encode-aligned
precise cuts. Mime_group scoping is the gate — precision does
NOT apply to GIF or PDF (frame extraction is exact and PDF page
selection is exact by definition).

**Long-form video** routes to a separate `split-video-fargate`
Lambda (same `split` OperationType, different worker; routing
via `processing_class`). No schema-side discriminator beyond MIME
detection + `processing_class`.

`availability: planned` until the cross-repo Lambda support ships
([`vKI0CFDu`](https://trello.com/c/vKI0CFDu) + lambdas L1).
Dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until then.

Per ADR-0014.

silence_mode_audio.description:
Silence-detect cut mode for audio (`mode: silence` —
Cinavia/Resemble territory, server-side silence detection).
OUT of V1 (Lambda support not yet shipped); declared as a
future-work feature here so SDK consumers see the gate.
Picked up as a separate post-launch ticket.

cut_points.description:
Cut points in numeric seconds (float, sub-second allowed).
Array entries; NOT a comma-separated string. MUST be
strictly increasing (preflight-validated against probe);
each entry > 0 and < probed duration. `uniqueItems: true`
catches accidental duplicates at the OpenAPI layer; strict
monotonicity and the < probed-duration bound are
preflight-checked at workflow-create. Output count =
`cut_points.length + 1`; `maxItems: 199` expresses the
200-output cap declaratively. Only valid when
`mode: cut_points`.

interval.description:
Cut interval in numeric seconds (float, sub-second
allowed). `ceil(probed_duration / interval) <= 200`
preflight rule applies. Only valid when `mode: interval`.

mode.description:
Split strategy:
- `interval`: cut every `interval` seconds.
- `count`: cut into `count` equal-duration pieces.
- `cut_points`: cut at explicit timestamps.

precision.description:
Cut precision. Under `fast`, audio cuts are
packet-boundary approximate (drift bounded by codec
packet size — typically <= 23ms for AAC, <= 26ms for
MP3). Under `exact`, the Lambda re-encodes to align cuts
precisely at the requested timestamps (slower, larger
output, but sample-accurate). Avoid "keyframe" language
— that's video-only; audio drift is packet-boundary.

mode.description:
PDF split strategy:
- `page_range`: select pages via a range expression.
- `page_groups`: group N pages per output.

page_groups.description:
Group N pages per output (e.g. `page_groups: 5` on a
50-page PDF produces 10 outputs of 5 pages each).
Required when `mode: page_groups`. The page-count cap
applies to the resolved group count: `ceil(total_pages /
page_groups) <= 200`.

page_range.description:
Page-range expression (e.g. "1-5", "1,3,5-7", "10-20").
1-based inclusive. Required when `mode: page_range`.
Resolved page count MUST be <= 200 (`invalid_options`
422 if exceeded — cross-ref
[`WgCqnMRa`](https://trello.com/c/WgCqnMRa)).

frame_range.description:
Frame range as `start-end` integer pair (e.g. "1-100").
REQUIRED in V1 — no silent default. Without explicit
range, a 30-second GIF at 24 fps = 720 frames would blow
the 200-output cap on every input. Both start and end are
1-based, inclusive. Resolved frame count MUST be <= 200
(`invalid_options` 422 if exceeded).

output_format.description:
Output image format for each extracted frame. Aspect ratio
and dimensions preserved from the source GIF.

count.description:
Number of equal-duration output pieces. Range 2..=200.
Only valid when `mode: count`.

cut_points.description:
Cut points in numeric seconds (float, sub-second allowed).
Array entries; NOT a comma-separated string. Strictly
increasing (preflight-validated against probe); each entry
> 0 and < probed duration. `uniqueItems: true` catches
accidental duplicates at the OpenAPI layer; strict
monotonicity and the < probed-duration bound are
preflight-checked at workflow-create. Output count =
`cut_points.length + 1`; `maxItems: 199` expresses the
200-output cap declaratively. Only valid when
`mode: cut_points`.

interval.description:
Cut interval in numeric seconds. `ceil(probed_duration /
interval) <= 200`. Only valid when `mode: interval`.

mode.description:
Split strategy:
- `interval`: cut every `interval` seconds.
- `count`: cut into `count` equal-duration pieces.
- `cut_points`: cut at explicit timestamps.

precision.description:
Cut precision. Under `fast`, video cuts are keyframe-
aligned (drift bounded by GOP size — typically up to 1-2
seconds depending on encoder). Under `exact`, the Lambda
re-encodes to align cuts precisely at the requested
timestamps (slower, larger output, but frame-accurate).

text_watermark.description:
Render a text overlay onto an image using bundled Liberation Sans
(SIL OFL). Single-input — text comes from options, not from a file
input. Source capped at 25 megapixels (V1 watermark precedent
retained). Per ADR-0004 §"V2 JobDefinition" + plan v5 §F1.

Two rendering modes:
- `single`: one label rendered at the anchor + margin position.
- `tiled`: text tiled across the source image (Cinavia / Adobe Stock
  pattern); rotation default -45° and tile_spacing controls density.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position. For watermark_mode: single, the text
label is placed at this anchor + margin_x/margin_y offset.
For watermark_mode: tiled, anchor is ignored (tiles fill the
source image).

color.description:
Text colour as hex RGB (#RRGGBB) or RGBA (#RRGGBBAA).
Default is white with 50% alpha.

font_family.description:
Font family. V2.0 ships with bundled Liberation Sans only;
user-uploaded fonts are a future extension (separate ticket).

font_size.description: Font size in pixels. Clamped to range.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px") or
percentage of base width (e.g. "5%"). Direction is
anchor-relative. Ignored when watermark_mode: tiled.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single only).
When the anchor is `center` the centre is the centre — there
is no anchor-relative direction. Non-zero `margin_x`
(anything other than the default `"0px"` or the equivalent
`"0%"`) combined with `anchor: center` is rejected by the
API at workflow-create time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).
To nudge a roughly-centred label, pick a peripheral anchor
(e.g. `top_left`) and use `margin_x` / `margin_y` to
position it. Not applicable to watermark_mode: tiled, where
anchor + margin are ignored entirely.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
base height. Ignored when watermark_mode: tiled.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single only).
Same rejection rule as `margin_x` — non-zero `margin_y` with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

opacity.description:
Text overlay opacity. Default 1.0 — the alpha channel of
`color` already encodes transparency, so opacity is usually
left at 1.0 and transparency is set via the color hex.

rotation.description:
Rotation angle in degrees. Default -45° suits tiled mode
(angled diagonal pattern). Clamped to range. Tiled canvas is
capped at 25 megapixels pre-rotation with 5000 px maximum
side length.

text.description:
Watermark text. Must be non-empty. Rendered with bundled
Liberation Sans (SIL OFL). A single text overlay is capped
at 4 megapixels before rendering fails.

tile_spacing.description:
Spacing between tiled labels in pixels. Defaults to font_size
when omitted. Only applies when watermark_mode is tiled.

watermark_mode.description:
Rendering mode:
- `single`: one label placed at the anchor + margin offset.
- `tiled`: repeated tiles across the entire source image at
  the rotation angle.

thumbnail.description: Generate a preview image from the source file

page.description: 1-based index of the printed page to render, after print-layout pagination is applied. For office formats this is the page in the LibreOffice PDF export — NOT a 1:1 sheet/slide index: a spreadsheet sheet may span multiple printed pages and multi-sheet workbooks paginate their sheets in order. For PDF inputs it is the PDF page number directly.

source.description: page = render a specific page/slide, cover = extract cover image (EPUB only, ignored for other formats)

video_text_watermark.description:
Render a text overlay onto a base video using bundled Liberation
Sans (SIL OFL) via FFmpeg's `drawtext` filter. Single-input — the
text and its styling come from options, not from a file input.
Per ADR-0013.

**Dedicated operation, not an extension of `text_watermark`.**
`text_watermark` renders onto images (single-pass Rust path);
`video_text_watermark` renders per-frame via FFmpeg drawtext +
re-encode (overlay = pixel modification, no stream-copy escape).
Different runtime, different deploy unit.

**Audio passthrough.** The base video's audio stream is preserved
unchanged via FFmpeg stream-copy — the watermark applies to the
visual track only.

Two rendering modes (mirrors `text_watermark`):
- `single`: one label rendered at the anchor + margin position.
- `tiled`: text tiled across the source frames at the rotation
  angle.

`availability: planned` until the cross-repo Lambda support ships;
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until then. Per
ADR-0013 + lambdas pre-launch epic Wave A (ticket
[`4NrRPCgh`](https://trello.com/c/4NrRPCgh)).

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position. For `watermark_mode: single`, the
text label is placed at this anchor + margin_x/margin_y
offset on each frame. For `watermark_mode: tiled`, anchor
is ignored (tiles fill the frame).

color.description:
Text colour as hex RGB (#RRGGBB) or RGBA (#RRGGBBAA).
Default is white with 50% alpha. Same vocabulary as
`text_watermark.image.color`.

font_size.description: Font size in pixels. Clamped to range.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px")
or percentage of video frame width (e.g. "5%").
Anchor-relative. Ignored when `watermark_mode: tiled`.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single
only). Non-zero `margin_x` combined with `anchor: center`
is rejected by the API at workflow-create time as
`invalid_options` (HTTP 422).

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
video frame height. Ignored when `watermark_mode: tiled`.

**`anchor: center` exception** (watermark_mode: single
only). Same rejection rule as `margin_x`.

opacity.description:
Text overlay opacity. Default 1.0 — the alpha channel of
`color` already encodes transparency, so opacity is
usually left at 1.0 and transparency is set via the color
hex.

rotation.description:
Rotation angle in degrees. Default -45° suits tiled mode
(angled diagonal pattern). Clamped to range.

text.description:
Watermark text. Must be non-empty. Rendered with bundled
Liberation Sans (SIL OFL).

tile_spacing.description:
Spacing between tiled labels in pixels. Defaults to
font_size when omitted. Only applies when watermark_mode
is tiled.

watermark_mode.description:
Rendering mode:
- `single`: one label placed at the anchor + margin offset
  on each frame.
- `tiled`: repeated tiles across the entire frame at the
  rotation angle.

video_watermark.description:
Apply an image overlay (file or external source) onto a base video
asset using FFmpeg's `overlay` filter. Multi-input role-based:
exactly one input with `role: base` (the source video) + exactly
one with `role: overlay` (the watermark image). The overlay can be
an upload, an external_import handle, or a vault connection per
`WorkflowSource`.

**Dedicated operation, not an extension of `image_watermark`.**
`image_watermark` is pure-Rust/no-FFmpeg and handles
image-on-image overlay only. `video_watermark` requires FFmpeg's
overlay filter + re-encode + a different deploy unit (no
stream-copy escape — overlay = pixel modification). Per ADR-0013.

**Audio passthrough.** The base video's audio stream is preserved
unchanged via FFmpeg stream-copy — the watermark applies to the
visual track only.

`availability: planned` until the cross-repo Lambda support ships;
dispatch returns `feature_not_available` (422) until then. Per
ADR-0013 + lambdas pre-launch epic Wave A (ticket
[`SlluxMBN`'s sibling `4NrRPCgh`](https://trello.com/c/4NrRPCgh)).

multi_overlay_stack.description:
Allow up to 8 overlay inputs per job (currently capped at 1
overlay). Per-overlay placement via JobInputV2.per_input_options.
Lambda-side support not yet confirmed; tagged planned per
ADR-0001 §1.4. Mirrors the same-named feature on
`image_watermark`.

anchor.description:
9-grid anchor position on each video frame. Same vocabulary
as `image_watermark.image` and `text_watermark.image`.

margin_x.description:
Horizontal offset from the anchor as pixels (e.g. "40px")
or percentage of video frame width (e.g. "5%"). Direction
is anchor-relative — positive moves toward the frame
centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** When the anchor is `center`
the centre is the centre — there is no anchor-relative
direction. Non-zero `margin_x` combined with
`anchor: center` is rejected by the API at workflow-create
time as `invalid_options` (HTTP 422). Same rule as the
other watermark operations.

margin_y.description:
Vertical offset from the anchor as pixels or percentage of
video frame height. Anchor-relative; positive moves toward
the frame centre.

**`anchor: center` exception.** Same rejection rule as
`margin_x`.

opacity.description:
Overlay opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque).
Clamped to range.

overlay_width.description:
Optional overlay width as pixels or percentage of video
frame width. Aspect ratio preserved. Omit to use the
original overlay size.
